english: the signs of the cases: ṅa (nga) miṅ (ming) tsám-gyi (tsam gyi) dgé-sloṅ (dge slong) ma (ma) yin (yin) Dzl. I am Bhikshu not only by name, I am not merely called so; da (da) tsám-gyi (tsam gyi) bár-du (bar du) Dzl. till about the present time (standing here rather pleon., as frq. is the case); brám-ze (bram ze) yčíg (gcig) tsám-gyi (tsam gyi) slád-du (slad du) Dzl. for the sake of a single Brahmin; spu (spu) nyág-ma (nyag ma) tsám-gyi (tsam gyi) 'gyód-pai ('gyod pa'i) sems (sems) Dzl. but a whit (lit. a little hair) of repentance. — 4. tsám-na (tsam na) referring to time: about a certain time, at the time when, when: nam-pýed (nam phyed) tsám-na (tsam na) about midnight; de (de) tsám-na (tsam na) then, at that time; esp. with verbs: kyím-du (khyim du) pýin (phyin) tsám-na (tsam na) Dzl. when he came home; inst. of tsám-na (tsam na) it is very common to say tsá-na (tsa na); byéd-gin (byed gin) yod (yod) tsá-na (tsa na) as he was just doing it Glr.; ɣnyid (gnyid) sad (sad) tsá-na (tsa na) when he awoke Glr.; zlá-ba (zla ba) brgyad (brgyad) soṅ (song) tsá-na (tsa na) when eight months had passed Glr.; esp. col.: *yoṅ (yong) tsá-na* (tsa na) W. as we came, on our journey hither, when incorr. *tsa-ne (tsa ne) (or sá-ne (sa ne))* is said, which is justifiable only in such cases, as: *ă-ma (a ma) kyé-sa-ne* (skye sa ne) from one's birth; ji-tsam-na (ji tsam na) or -nas (-nas) when, yet mostly pleon., in as far as the sentence beginning with ji-tsam-na (ji tsam na) after all concludes with nas (nas), pa (pa) daṅ (dang), dus-kyi (dus kyi) tse (tshe) etc., v. Feer Introd. frq., also Tar. — 5. tsám-du (tsam du) denoting extent, degree, intensity: as far as, about so far, nearly up to, even to, till, so that, and tsam (tsam) in various other applications: lam (lam) pýed (phyed) tsám-du (tsam du) about half way; frq. with verbs: bá-spu (ba spu) laṅs-pa (langs pa) tsám-du (tsam du) skrags (skrags) Dzl. he was so frightened, that his hair stood on end; dúm-bur (dum bur) bcád-pa (bcad pa) tsám-du (tsam du) sdug-bsṅál-gyis (sdug bsngal gyis) ydúṅs-te (gdungs te) Dzl. tormented by a pain, as if he were cut to pieces; bus (bus) ma (ma) mtóṅ-ba (mthong ba) tsám-du (tsam du) dgá-ste (dga' ste) Dzl. 'being glad even to a mother's being seen by her child', i.e. so glad as a child is, when beholding its mother again; sometimes tsám-la (tsam la) for tsám-na (tsam na) and tsám-du (tsam du) Mil. yet not frq. and more col.: ḍib (grib) tsám-la (tsam la) in the shade; *śiṅ-ni (shing ni) tsám-la* (tsam la) W. under, before, near a tree; tsám-gyis (tsam gyis) instrum.: ṅan-ṅon (ngan ngon) tsám-gyis (tsam gyis) čóg-śes-pa (chog shes pa) content with every thing, as poor as it may be; com. added to the inf.: smrás-pa (smras pa) tsám-du (tsam du) as soon as it had been said Dzl. frq., or also: 'in the mere saying so' Stg.; inst. of it, col.: *zer (zer) tsam (tsam) žíg-la* (zhig la); W.: *zér-ra (zer ra) tsám-žíg-ga* (tsam zhig ga). — tsam (tsam) yaṅ (yang) with a following negative: not the least, mós-pa (mos pa) tsam (tsam) yaṅ (yang) mi (mi) byéd-pa (byed pa) Mil. to pay not the least respect; not in the least, not at all: nyí-ma (nyi ma) daṅ (dang) zlá-ba (zla ba) tsam (tsam) yaṅ (yang) ltar (ltar) med (med) Dzl. neither sun nor moon is to be seen at all. — tsám-pa (tsam pa) adj., mi-tsad-tsám-pa (mi tshad tsam pa) man-sized, having the size of a man Tar. — tsám-po (tsam po) Mil. mi (mi) tsám-po (tsam po) yóṅs-kyi (yongs kyi) sems-la (sems la) 'júg ('jug) prob : I shall enter into the soul of the very first man I meet with; also = gaṅ (gang) (cf. rtags (rtags) gaṅ (gang) yód-pa-la (yod pa la) above). — Cs. has besides: tsám-po-ba (tsam po ba) a comparing, estimating; tsam-po'i (tsam po'i) tsig (tshig) a comparative expression; tsám-po'i (tsam po'i) don (don) a comparative sense(?). II. used interrogatively: how much? how many? *rin (rin) tsam?* (tsam) W. how dear?

1. v. tsam (tsam) towards end of preced. article. — 2. sbst. flour from parched barley, v. rtsám-pa (rtsam pa). — 3. n. of a country Tar. 10, 14; 20, 16; acc. to Ssk. Lexx. = Bhagalpore, v. Köpp. I, 96; in modern geography: the small Hindu mountain-province Chamba on the river Ravi, under British protection.
tripping to and fro, fidgeting about W. (cf. tsab-tsób (tsab tsob)).
n. of a place, freq. resorted to by Mil.
Ssk. 1. a painted mark on the forehead, being the badge of various sects Sch. — 2. name of several plants, esp. Ricinus communis, so perh. Lt.; in Lh.: Anemone rivularis, common there.
China Cs.; now com. ma-ha-tsín (ma ha tsin).

mouse C., tsi-ghi (tsi ghi) id. Ts.; tsi-čuṅ (tsi chung) shrew (mouse) Sch.; táṅ-gi (tang gi) tsi-tsí (tsi tsi) field-mouse Schr.; sai (sa'i) tsi-tsí (tsi tsi) mole Schr.; tsi-tsis-'dzin (tsi tsis 'dzin) n. of a plant Wdn.
